reply.draft_reply: Sorry you're locked out. I've flagged your account for a manual reset — you'll get a fresh link within a few minutes so you make your standup.
Thanks for the suggestion! Recurring tasks are on our radar — I've added your vote to the request so the product team sees the demand.
Sorry about the double charge. I've escalated the duplicate $49 charge from June 3rd to billing for a refund — you'll get a confirmation within one business day.
Thanks for the clear report — the empty assignee column in CSV exports is a bug on our side. I've filed it with the export team; as a workaround, the XLSX export includes assignees correctly.
Yes — open the task, click the '...' menu and choose 'Move to project'. The task keeps its comments and history. I'll flag the docs gap so it's easier to find.

Thank you for the kind words — and you're right to be frustrated that integrations keep breaking. I've reopened the Slack notification issue with high priority and asked an engineer to follow up with you directly today.
Thank you so much — made our day! I've passed your note along to the design team.
Sorry — a 500 on bulk creation blocking your launch is serious. I've escalated to the API team as urgent; in the meantime batches of 150 or fewer succeed reliably.
Good catch — if the task list shows 14 done, the burndown should too. This looks like a chart aggregation bug rather than a misreading; I've filed it with repro details from your sprint.
Sorry to see you go. I've started the cancellation and the data-deletion request per our privacy policy — you'll receive a confirmation email for both within 24 hours.
Sorry you're still locked out. I've triggered a manual credential reset on our side — a fresh sign-in link is on its way so you're back on the board before standup.
Automations live under Project Settings → Automations. Pick the 'task moves to Done' trigger and the 'notify channel' action — your example takes about a minute to set up.
That's on us — a rule should never touch tasks without due dates. I've escalated for an immediate board restore from last night's snapshot and disabled the rule until we confirm the fix.

Thanks for the report — duplicate firing on status changes is a known issue with the new trigger engine and a fix is rolling out this week. Re-enabling the rule after the update stops the double posts.
Scheduled triggers aren't in this release — Automations are event-based for now. I've logged your Friday-archive use case as a request; it's a popular one.
Automations are web-only in this release — the iOS app will get the tab in an upcoming update. Rules you create on web still run for everyone, including mobile users.
A run is counted only when a rule's trigger fires and its action executes — a rule matching zero tasks doesn't consume quota. Your usage is visible under Settings → Automations → Usage.
Sorry about that — the Automations add-on shouldn't appear unless enabled by an admin. I've flagged the $19 line item to billing for removal and refund, and asked them to audit how it was activated.
Sorry — the preview undercounting affected tasks is a serious bug. I've escalated label restoration for your 40+ tasks and filed the preview issue with the Automations team.
Sorry this is happening again. I've triggered a manual reset and linked your previous ticket so the auth team investigates why resets keep failing for your account.
Fantastic to hear the Monday rule replaced the manual cloning! Checklist copying is coming in the next Automations update — your rule will pick it up automatically.
